title: Memory Cycle Part II: Key Features of a Cycle
title_translation: 存储周期（下）：周期的关键特征

paragraphs.source: The memory industry has been defined by commoditization, which comes with cyclicality. This outcome reflects a combination of industry-wide competitive behavior, recurring lapses in capital discipline, and the nature of DRAM scaling we explained earlier.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The memory industry has been defined by commoditization, which comes with cyclicality. This outcome reflects a combination of industry-wide competitive behavior, recurring lapses in capital discipline, and the nature of DRAM scaling we explained earlier.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The memory industry has been defined by commoditization, which comes with cyclicality. This outcome reflects a combination of industry-wide competitive behavior, recurring lapses in capital discipline, and the nature of DRAM scaling we explained earlier.

pro.text: 存储行业向来以大宗商品化为核心特征，周期性亦随之而来。这种局面的形成，是全行业竞争博弈、资本纪律屡遭抛弃，以及前文所述 DRAM 微缩物理本质等多重因素交织的结果。

applied_terms: Business terminology requires precision: 'commoditization' (大宗商品化/通用化), 'capital discipline' (资本纪律), 'pure bit growth' (纯比特增长/单纯追求容量增长).
Ensure 'structurally tighter supply' (结构性供应趋紧) is emphasized as a core recurring theme.

understanding.role_in_article: Explains the economic and behavioral mechanics of memory cycles, highlighting how recent industry trauma led to the structurally tight supply of the current era.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Shifts focus from the physical/technical constraints of DRAM scaling to its economic and behavioral consequences (commoditization).
understanding.relation_to_next: Lays the 'structurally tighter supply' baseline, which the AI/HBM factors will dramatically exacerbate in the core thesis section.
key_points: Memory cyclicality is driven by commoditization, competitive behavior, and lapses in capital discipline.
The previous cycle reset industry expectations, forcing memory suppliers to prioritize disciplined capex and high-margin products over pure bit growth.
Customers now recognize supply chain fragility, laying the foundation for a structurally tighter supply.

issues.description: 术语翻译不一致。本文中对“bit”的翻译在“比特”与“位元”之间反复横跳（如同一段落出现“比特售价”与“位元供应”，以及第7、8、9、46段均使用了“位元”）。此外，系统提示已明确要求将“pure bit growth”译为“纯比特增长/单纯追求容量增长”，术语表也规定了“Bit output → 比特产出”。
将知名半导体市场研究机构“Integrated Circuit Engineering (ICE)”直译为了“集成电路（integrated circuit）工程公司”。
“greenfield”在半导体与制造业语境中指“全新厂址建设/绿地项目”，译文处理为“新建（绿地）”略显生硬，带有翻译腔。
中英文之间缺少必要的空格（如“1c节点”、“1a节点”）。

paragraphs.source: At its core, memory’s cyclicality is driven by timing mismatches between demand changes and corresponding supply responses. Aside from the buffer of short-term inventories, DRAM supply is not very flexible. It can take years to bring meaningful new DRAM supply online, trying to meet demand that fluctuates daily.

pro.text: 究其根本，存储周期的驱动力在于需求变化与供应响应之间的时间错配。若抛开短期库存的缓冲作用不谈，DRAM 的供应弹性极低。要让实质性的新增 DRAM 产能上线往往耗时数年，而市场需求却瞬息万变，二者极难同频。

paragraphs.source: Memory manufacturing, much like logic, is among the most capital-intensive industries in the world. Building leading-edge DRAM and NAND fabs requires multi-billion-dollar investments (which have steadily increased over the past few decades), multi-year construction timelines, extended yield-learning curves across successive process nodes, and lengthy ramp-up periods before meaningful volume production is achieved.

pro.text: 与逻辑芯片类似，存储制造稳居全球资本最密集行业之列。建设先进制程的 DRAM 和 NAND 晶圆厂需要砸下数十亿美元（且该数字在过去数十年间持续攀升）。不仅建设周期长达数年，跨代制程节点的良率学习曲线极为漫长，在实现实质性量产前，还需经历旷日持久的产能爬坡期。

paragraphs.source: These heavy capital investments mean that suppliers need to operate with high utilization rates to generate cash profits to earn a payback. However, utilization rates ultimately depend on market demand, which is influenced by a range of external factors (macroeconomy, end market sentiment, product cycle, etc). In practice, utilization can swing dramatically across cycles, ranging from roughly 95% in a supercycle to as low as 50% in significant downcycles.

pro.text: 如此沉重的资本开支，意味着供应商必须维持高利用率运转，方能创造现金利润以收回投资。然而，利用率最终取决于市场需求，而需求又受制于诸多外部因素（宏观经济、终端市场情绪、产品周期等）。在实际运作中，利用率在不同周期内波动剧烈：在超级周期中可飙升至 95% 左右，而在严重的下行周期中则会一路陡降至 50%。

pro.text: 尽管如此，由于大部分成本已沦为沉没成本（晶圆厂已落成，设备已就位），只要比特售价能覆盖现金运营成本，供应商继续流片生产晶圆就是更优解。当需求不及比特供应时，价格自然应声下跌。至于市场内部的需求弹性，则历来是业内争论的焦点。

paragraphs.source: Memory supply can be expanded by migrating to more advanced process nodes with yield improvements, which increases bit supply without requiring new “greenfield” wafer capacity additions. For example, Samsung’s leading-edge 1c DRAM process node delivers roughly ~70% higher bits output per wafer compared with its 1a node. This means that on a per-wafer basis, a 1c-node DRAM wafer can deliver roughly 70% higher bit output than a 1a-node wafer (assuming the same yield), meaning significantly more memory supply from the same amount of raw material.

pro.text: 供应商还可通过向更先进制程节点迭代并提升良率来扩张存储供应。这种方式无需投建全新的“绿地”晶圆产能，即可直接提升比特供应。以三星（Samsung）为例，其最前沿的 1c 节点 DRAM 制程与 1a 节点相比，单片晶圆的比特产出提升了约 70%。这意味着在同等良率假设下，单片 1c 节点 DRAM 晶圆的比特产出比 1a 节点高出约 70%；换言之，消耗同等规模的原材料，却能产出丰厚得多的存储供应。

paragraphs.source: The impact of node migration, however, is dynamic over time. When a new node is first introduced, initial yields are typically lower, limiting effective output as well as wafer capacity, given the potential new equipment introduction, replacement, and ramp-up timeline. As yield learning progresses and node migration expands, the bit output per wafer increases materially, resulting in more bit supply even if wafer output is held constant.

pro.text: 然而，节点演进的影响会随时间动态变化。首次引入新制程节点时，初期良率通常较低。考虑到潜在的新设备引入、替换以及产能爬坡时间表，这会限制有效产出与晶圆产能。随着良率不断改善且节点演进深入，单片晶圆的比特产出将大幅提升；此时即使晶圆产出保持不变，也会带来更多位元供应。

paragraphs.source: In a downturn, the impact of pricing declines can be existential for memory suppliers. By the time pricing rolls over, manufacturers have already committed and deployed multi-billion-dollar capital investments into fabs and equipment that cannot be economically idled. As demand weakens, utilization rates fall, fixed costs are under-absorbed, and cash generation deteriorates rapidly. The result is a sharp compression in gross margins and an inability to earn an adequate return on invested capital precisely when balance-sheet stress is rising.

pro.text: 步入下行周期后，价格暴跌对存储供应商而言往往是生死攸关的。当价格见顶回落时，制造商早已砸下数十亿美元的资本支出（capex），将其部署在晶圆厂与设备上；强行闲置这些资产极不划算。随着需求疲软，利用率随之下滑，固定成本未充分吸收，现金流创造能力迅速恶化。最终结果是毛利率急剧收缩，且恰逢资产负债表压力飙升之际，企业根本无法获得足够的投资资本回报率。

paragraphs.source: During the Windows PC supercycle of the early to mid-1990s, there were approximately 20 meaningful DRAM suppliers. Elevated demand and strong pricing attracted aggressive capital investment and new entrants. Subsequent downcycles systematically eliminated the weaker players. From roughly 20+ players in the mid-1990s, the number of players contracted to the mid-teens in the 2000s and early 2010s, to fewer than 10 relevant suppliers in the 2020s. Today, there are only 3-4 material suppliers.

pro.text: 在二十世纪九十年代初中期的 Windows 个人电脑 (PC) 超级周期期间，市场上约有 20 家具备规模的 DRAM 供应商。高涨的需求与强劲的价格吸引了激进的资本投资与新入局者。随后的下行周期对弱势玩家进行了系统性出清。从九十年代中期的 20 多家厂商，到本世纪初及 2010 年代初萎缩至十五六家，再到 2020 年代相关供应商已不足 10 家。时至今日，真正具备影响力的供应商仅剩 3 到 4 家。

paragraphs.source: From a demand perspective, memory consumption is not always linear or predictable. During mature phases of existing product cycles, demand growth can be relatively stable, driven primarily by incremental unit growth or stable increases in memory content per device. However, during “inflection periods,” when new computing platforms or architectures emerge as primary demand drivers, memory demand can shift abruptly. During these periods, memory consumption tends to grow non-linearly if not explosively.

pro.text: 从需求端来看，存储消耗并不总是线性或可预测的。在现有产品周期的成熟阶段，需求增长相对平稳，主要由终端出货量的增量或单机存储容量的稳步提升来驱动。然而，一旦进入“拐点期”——即全新计算平台或架构跃升为核心需求驱动力时，存储需求便会发生剧变。在此期间，存储消耗往往呈非线性甚至爆炸式增长。

New computing platforms like the PC, the smartphone, cloud computing, and now AI accelerators drove abrupt increases in both the number of systems and memory content per system. In prior cycles, these demand inflections often caught memory suppliers off guard, a dynamic we will discuss in the following section.

pro.text: 过去几十年间，这类产品周期拐点曾多次出现。PC、智能手机、云计算以及如今的 AI 加速器等全新计算平台，双重驱动了系统数量与单机存储容量的陡增。在过往周期中，这些需求拐点往往让存储供应商措手不及，我们将在下一节深入探讨这一动态。

paragraphs.source: However, such inflection-driven upcycles have not been sustainable in the long run. Prior memory supercycles have tended to peak and roll into downcycles within one or two years, as elevated profitability drives aggressive capital investment, accelerated capacity expansion, and faster-than-anticipated bit supply growth. These supply responses, combined with the inherently cyclical nature of end demand, have consistently led to oversupply and subsequent market corrections.

pro.text: 然而，从长远来看，这类由拐点驱动的上行周期往往难以持久。过往的存储超级周期通常在一两年内便会触顶并滑入下行周期，因为高企的利润会刺激激进的资本投资、加速的产能扩张以及超预期的位元供应增长。这些供应端反应，叠加终端需求固有的周期性特质，必然导致供过于求及随之而来的市场回调。

paragraphs.source: During the 1993 memory supercycle, the DRAM industry entered a strong upcycle driven by the rapid adoption of Windows PCs and the broad implementation of graphical operating systems. Unlike prior generations of personal computers, Windows PCs transitioned from text-based interfaces to GUI (Graphical User Interface)-driven computing, which dramatically increased DRAM requirements per system. Average DRAM content per PC jumped from roughly 1–2MB to 4–8MB, representing an approximate 4× increase in memory content per device. This step-function increase in DRAM intensity coincided with accelerating PC adoption, with unit shipments growing at roughly double-digit rates.

pro.text: 在 1993 年的存储超级周期中，随着 Windows PC 的快速普及与图形操作系统的广泛应用，DRAM 行业步入强劲的上行周期。与前几代个人电脑不同，Windows PC 从基于文本的界面转向由图形用户界面 (GUI) 驱动的计算模式，这使得单机 DRAM 需求激增。每台 PC 的平均 DRAM 容量从约 1 至 2MB 跃升至 4 至 8MB，单机存储容量实现了约 4 倍的增长。DRAM 搭载量的这种阶梯式跃升，恰逢 PC 普及加速，系统出货量也保持着约两位数的增速。

paragraphs.source: On the supply side, the industry was emerging from a prolonged downturn in the late 1980s because of intensified competition, declining margins, and a significant shakeout among U.S. and European DRAM suppliers. As a result, capacity expansion had been constrained and yields were uneven, leaving the supply suppliers ill-prepared to absorb the sudden surge in demand. With this perfect setup in both supply and demand, an industry shortage inevitably occurred. During 1993 and 1994, DRAM demand outpaced supply despite most fabs running at full utilization. Spot and contract prices for 4Mb and 16Mb DRAM rose sharply, and gross margins for leading suppliers surged well above 50%.

pro.text: 在供应端，由于竞争白热化、利润率下滑，加之欧美 DRAM 供应商经历了一轮大洗牌，整个行业在 20 世纪 80 年代末刚从漫长的低谷期中复苏。因此，产能扩张处处受限，良率参差不齐，导致供应商面对突如其来的需求激增时措手不及。供需两端如此完美的共振，令全行业的短缺成为必然。1993 至 1994 年间，尽管大多数晶圆厂都在满负荷运转，DRAM 依然供不应求。4Mb 和 16Mb DRAM 的现货与合约价格一路狂飙，头部供应商的毛利率更是飙升至 50% 以上。

paragraphs.source: Unsurprisingly, this set the stage for a downturn. Japanese incumbents and then-emerging Korean players responded with aggressive capacity expansion—bringing new fabs online and accelerating process shrinks. This was reflected in global semiconductor manufacturing spend as the supercycle took hold, with capex as a percentage of semiconductor production rising steadily and at one point exceeding 30%. Reinforcing this late-cycle signal, roughly 50 fab construction plans were announced during 1995–1996 alone.

pro.text: 毫无意外，这也为随后的下行周期埋下了伏笔。日本老牌巨头与当时正崛起的韩国厂商纷纷以激进的产能扩张予以回应——新晶圆厂密集投产，工艺微缩进程全面提速。随着超级周期步入高潮，全球半导体制造支出也印证了这一点：资本支出占半导体产值的比例稳步攀升，一度突破 30%。仅在 1995 至 1996 年间，业内就宣布了约 50 项晶圆厂建设计划，进一步坐实了这一周期尾声的危险信号。

paragraphs.source: The 2010 memory supercycle started in roughly the 2nd half of 2009, driven by two simultaneous demand inflections: (1) the smartphone rapid adoption globally, led by the iPhone and a rapidly proliferating Android ecosystem, and (2) the early hyperscaler buildout wave (e.g., Google, Amazon, Facebook). Server DRAM intensity increased due to virtualization and scale-out services. Supply growth was muted as suppliers drastically cut investment in the poor macro environment post the Global Financial Crisis.

pro.text:
2010 年的存储器超级周期大约始于 2009 年下半年，受两大需求拐点双重驱动：
1. 由 iPhone 及迅速扩张的 Android 生态系统引领的全球智能手机快速普及
2. 早期超大规模云服务商（如谷歌、亚马逊、Facebook）的建设浪潮
受虚拟化和横向扩展服务推动，服务器 DRAM 密度不断提升。同时，在全球金融危机后的恶劣宏观环境下，供应商大幅削减投资，导致供应增长疲软。

paragraphs.source: On top of this, smartphone demand growth was a near-vertical volume step-up. Global smartphonec by broader Android price-tier penetration and iPhone scaling. On the cloud side, while the servers’ shipment growth increased much more slowly compared to mobile during that period, the key change was the DRAM content per server. The industry was shifting toward higher-memory configurations to support consolidation, virtualization density, and early big-data workloads. DRAM content per server increased from single-digit gigabytes (GBs) to tens of GBs, representing a significant step-function increase in memory intensity per system.

pro.text: 在此之上，智能手机需求增长呈现近乎垂直的阶梯式跃升。随着 Android 阵营在各价位段的广泛渗透以及 iPhone 规模的不断扩大，全球智能手机销量激增。在云端，尽管该时期服务器出货量的增速远不及移动设备，但核心变化在于单台服务器的 DRAM 容量。整个行业正向更高内存配置演进，以支持资源整合、虚拟化密度以及早期的大数据工作负载。单台服务器的 DRAM 容量从个位数吉字节（GB）飙升至数十 GB，标志着单系统内存密度的显著阶梯式跃升。

paragraphs.source: Similar to prior cycles, the sudden demand surge combined with constrained supply drove a meaningful increase in DRAM pricing. What differentiated the 2010 cycle, however, was the rapid rise of LPDDR within the overall DRAM mix as mobile demand scaled. Because LPDDR products are mostly more cost- and price-efficient and are sold into more price-sensitive end markets, the resulting pricing uplift was materially less dramatic than prior PC-driven supercycles. The pricing characteristics of LPDDR have remained structurally consistent to this day.

pro.text: 与以往周期类似，需求骤增与供应受限双重作用，推动 DRAM 价格大幅上涨。然而，2010 年周期的不同之处在于，随着移动端需求爆发，LPDDR 在整体 DRAM 结构中的占比迅速攀升。由于 LPDDR 产品大多在成本和定价上更具效率，且面向对价格更敏感的终端市场，这导致其价格涨幅明显不及此前由个人电脑 (PC) 驱动的超级周期。LPDDR 的这一价格特征在结构上一直延续至今。

paragraphs.source: Moreover, DRAM pricing in this cycle peaked earlier and rolled over more quickly than in previous upcycles. Pricing momentum proved difficult to sustain even within the nominal upcycle window. The DDR3 2Gb contract pricing fell ~46% from its 1H10 peak of ~$46.5 to ~$25 by November 2010. By 2011, the correction had broadened further, with a further correction of DRAM pricing.

pro.text: 此外，本轮周期中 DRAM 价格见顶更早，且回落速度快于以往的上行周期。即使在名义上的上行周期窗口内，价格上涨动能也难以维系。DDR3 2Gb 合约价从 2010 年上半年的约 46.5 美元高点，暴跌约 46% 至 2010 年 11 月的约 25 美元。进入 2011 年，回调范围进一步扩大，DRAM 价格持续下探。

paragraphs.source: Another key development of this cycle was the rapid standardization of mobile DRAM which further accelerated commoditization. LPDDR2 was standardized by JEDEC in 2009, pushing mobile DRAM into a tightly specified, consumer-oriented market structure that limited supplier differentiation and therefore pricing power. These factors together only compressed the length of the upcycles and time to reach the downcycle starting roughly in 2nd half of 2010 and following years.

pro.text: 本轮周期的另一项关键进展是移动端 DRAM 的快速标准化，这进一步加速了其通用化进程。2009 年，JEDEC (固态技术协会) 制定了 LPDDR2 标准，将移动端 DRAM 推入规格严苛、面向消费者的市场结构，限制了供应商的差异化空间，进而削弱了其定价权。这些因素共同压缩了上行周期的长度，并缩短了进入下行周期的时间，导致下行周期在大约 2010 年下半年及随后几年提前到来。

paragraphs.source: The 2017–2018 memory supercycle is still a fresh memory for many in the industry and is oft-cited as a good analogue for today’s supercycle. On the demand side, the key driver was server upgrades and buildouts, driven by virtualization, scale-out architectures, and increasingly memory-intensive workloads. DRAM content per server increased materially, shifting demand toward higher-capacity configurations. Server DRAM also enjoys higher ASP and margins compared to PC and mobile, and is therefore more profitable for suppliers.

pro.text: 业内许多人对 2017 至 2018 年的存储器超级周期依然记忆犹新，该周期也常被视为当前超级周期的绝佳参照。在需求端，核心驱动力在于服务器的升级与扩建，这主要受虚拟化、横向扩展架构以及内存密集度日益增加的工作负载所推动。单台服务器的 DRAM 容量大幅增加，促使需求向大容量配置转移。相较于 PC 和移动端，服务器 DRAM 享有更高的平均售价 (ASP) 和利润率，因此能为供应商带来更丰厚的利润。

paragraphs.source: Yet, as with the prior cycles, the fundamental cyclical dynamics of the industry prevailed. A few familiar factors developed in late 2018 and 2019: (1) supply growth re-accelerated as manufacturers responded to elevated pricing with aggressive capacity additions and technology ramps, and (2) demand growth normalized as end markets absorbed excess inventory and hyperscaler purchasing slowed. Oversupply drove pricing down, and the cycle spiraled downwards.

然而，与以往周期一样，行业底层的周期性规律依然发挥了主导作用。2018 年底至 2019 年，几个熟悉的因素再度浮现：
(1) 面对高企的价格，制造商激进扩充产能并加速技术爬坡，导致供应增长重新加速；
(2) 随着终端市场消化过剩库存，加上超大规模云服务商采购放缓，需求增长回归常态。
供过于求迫使价格下行，整个周期随之螺旋式坠落。

paragraphs.source: The most recent DRAM upcycle was catalyzed during the COVID-era “chip crisis,” an unprecedented synchronization of demand shock and supply disruption. Global lockdowns caused abrupt shifts in end markets, including work-from-home, remote education, cloud usage, and digital entertainment. These unexpected social changes drove a sudden surge in demand for PCs, servers, networking equipment, and consumer electronics. All of these categories are DRAM-intensive, and no pre-pandemic forecast could have reasonably foreseen the changes.

pro.text: 距离当前最近的一次 DRAM 上行周期，是由新冠疫情时期的“芯片危机”催生的。这是一场史无前例的需求冲击与供应中断的同步爆发。全球封锁导致终端市场需求突变，居家办公、远程教育、云服务及数字娱乐全面爆发。这些始料未及的社会变革，直接引爆了个人电脑、服务器、网络设备和消费电子产品的需求狂潮。上述所有品类均高度依赖 DRAM，而疫情前的任何预测都绝无可能预见到这番剧变。

paragraphs.source: On the supply side, the industry was structurally constrained. During the pandemic, suppliers faced additional friction from labor shortages, logistics disruptions, and delayed equipment deliveries. Even where capex budgets existed, wafer output could not be ramped quickly. At the same time, most suppliers had entered the pandemic period with a relatively disciplined supply posture following the painful 2018–2019 downturn, limiting their ability and willingness to add more capacity in response to short-term pricing signals.

paragraphs.source: Capex decisions during this period were therefore cautious and selective. Rather than aggressive greenfield expansion, memory suppliers prioritized node migration and productivity improvements within existing fabs. Advanced-node DRAM transitions were already becoming more complex and capital-intensive, with diminishing bit-growth returns per wafer as scaling challenges increased. This meant that even elevated capex levels translated into less incremental supply than in prior cycles. The pandemic-era upcycle thus reinforced a structural shift: supply growth became increasingly constrained not just by capital discipline, but by physics and process complexity.

pro.text: 因此，这一时期的资本支出决策极为谨慎且极具选择性。存储器供应商并未采取激进的新建（绿地）扩张，而是将现有晶圆厂内的节点演进与产能优化置于首位。先进节点 DRAM 的技术过渡本就愈发复杂且属于资本密集型，随着微缩难度攀升，单片晶圆的位元增长边际收益正在不断递减。这意味着，即便维持高额的资本支出，其转化为实际新增供应的规模也远不及以往周期。疫情时期的上行周期由此彻底坐实了一项结构性转变：供应增长的瓶颈不再仅仅是资本纪律，更在于物理极限与工艺复杂度的双重掣肘。

paragraphs.source: Crucially, this cycle reset industry behavior and expectations. Memory suppliers emerged with a stronger appreciation for disciplined capex, tighter inventory management, and the value of prioritizing higher-margin products over pure bit growth. Customers, meanwhile, recognized the fragility of semiconductor supply chains and the strategic importance of securing memory capacity. These dynamics laid the foundation for the current supercycle by creating a structurally tighter supply. In this sense, the COVID-era DRAM upcycle was not just a temporary dislocation, but a formative event that reshaped the memory industry’s supply-demand balance heading into the current cycle.

pro.text: 至关重要的是，本轮周期彻底重塑了行业的行为模式与预期。经历洗礼后，存储器供应商更加笃信严守资本纪律与精细化库存管理的必要性，并深刻意识到优先发展高利润率产品远比单纯追求位元增长更有价值。与此同时，客户也清醒地认识到半导体供应链的脆弱性，以及锁定存储器产能的战略意义。这些博弈与转变促成了结构性供应趋紧，为当前的超级周期埋下了伏笔。从这个意义上讲，疫情时期的 DRAM 上行周期绝非一次短暂的供需错配，而是一场深远的行业重构，它彻底改写了存储器行业的供需平衡，并直接引领我们步入当前的超级周期。
